DECLARATION OF JACQUELINE YEE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO PETITION OF ERIC JEFFRESS TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF GREGORY JEFFRESS BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION (TRANSACTION ID # 75422976) FILED BY DEFENDANT TRUITT AND WHITE LUMBER COMPANY

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, NOTICE OF HEARING RE: PETITION OF ERIC JEFFRESS TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF GREGORY JEFFRESS BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION. THE COURT STATES THAT IT RECEIVED A REQUEST FROM COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TRUITT & WHITE TO APPEAR TO ADDRESS THE UNTIMELY FILED OPPOSITION. NO APPEARANCE BY COUNSEL FOR TRUITT & WHITE. THE COURT STATES THAT THE OPPOSITION IS UNTIMELY, THAT THE OPPOSITION WAS NOT CONSIDERED, AND ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION OF ERIC JEFFRESS TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF GREGORY JEFFRESS BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 2035.010. COURT TO REVIEW THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE PETITIONER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED, BAILIFF: NOT PRESENT. (304/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Eric Jeffress To Perpetuate Testimony Of Gregory Jeffress By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing via Zoom for Petitioner Eric Jeffress;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Eric Jeffress To Perpetuate Testimony Of Gregory Jeffress By Videotaped Deposition.

The Court states that it received a request from counsel for Defendant Truitt & White to appear to address the untimely filed opposition. No appearance by counsel for Truitt & White when the matter was called. The Court states that the opposition is untimely, that the opposition was not considered, and adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Petition of Eric Jeffress to Perpetuate Testimony of Gregory Jeffress by Videotaped Deposition is GRANTED under C.C.P. 2035.010. Adverse parties were provided notice. The videotaped deposition of Gregory Jeffress will occur by Zoom technology within thirty days. All parties are to file a Special Notice of Appearance within two Court days.

Court to review the proposed order submitted by the petitioner.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not Reported, Bailiff: Not Present. (304/EPS)
